_comment: Merge the hooks block below into your project's .claude/settings.json. The three triggers are all generally available (no project-specific tooling). The optional domain trigger at the bottom is for higher-signal checkpointing if you have a state-machine-like event; delete it otherwise.

PreToolUse._comment: IMMUTABILITY GUARD: block direct edits to the append-only archive. All archive writes must go through `mem demote`. Fails open on malformed input, so it can never wedge the session.

PreCompact._comment: PRIMARY: fires right before auto and manual compaction. Empty matcher = both. CHECKPOINT_FORCE=1 bypasses the rate limit so this critical write never gets skipped. Keep CHECKPOINT_STATE_CMD fast — this hook blocks compaction until it returns.

SessionEnd._comment: CRASH BACKUP: PreCompact never fires if the session is killed/crashes. 'other' = abnormal termination. Async, cannot block.

Stop._comment: HEARTBEAT: fires once per agent turn, keeping the file fresh mid-session so PreCompact always has recent state. The rate limit (no FORCE here) smooths rapid turns. Swap for a PostToolUse Edit|Write matcher if you prefer triggering on file changes instead.
Stop._safety: This command MUST stay non-blocking (checkpoint.sh always exits 0). NEVER point a Stop hook at a command that exits 2 or returns decision:block to force the agent to 'consolidate/review' — that re-triggers the turn and loops. Consolidation is driven by the CLAUDE.md protocol inside normal turns, never by a blocking hook.

_optional_domain_trigger_PostToolUse._comment: OPTIONAL: also checkpoint on a meaningful state transition in your own tooling (an MCP tool call, a process step). Rename this key to 'PostToolUse' and set the matcher to your event to enable. Delete if you have none.
